vulnerability.description: The demo HTTP service concatenates the 'host' query parameter into a shell command executed with shell=True, enabling unauthenticated OS command injection and full read of process environment secrets.
vulnerability.root_cause: User-controlled 'host' query param is interpolated into cmd = f'echo pinging {host}' and passed to subprocess.run(..., shell=True) without sanitization.
vulnerability.attack_scenario: Attacker sends GET /api/ping?host=<payload> where payload breaks out of echo and runs arbitrary shell commands. The planted flag is in KAVACH_FLAG environment variable readable via printf or env.

primitives.name: shell_concat_sink
primitives.description: host query param concatenated into shell command string

primitives.name: env_flag_leak
primitives.description: KAVACH_FLAG env var available to subprocess inherits attacker-controlled shell

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: Concatenated into shell echo command — primary injection vector
parameters.encoding_notes: Try shell metacharacters: ; && | $() — patched twin blocks non-alphanumeric except .-

source_artifacts.patch_diff:
- cmd = f'echo pinging {host}'  # shell=True
+ if not all(c.isalnum() or c in '.-' for c in host): abort(400)

vulnerable_code_snippets.content:
cmd = f'echo pinging {host}'
out = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, env={'KAVACH_FLAG': flag})

patched_code_snippets.content:
if not all(c.isalnum() or c in '.-' for c in host):
    return self._send(400, 'invalid host')

success_signals.description: Captured flag wrapped in KAVACH capture marker in response body

failure_signals.description: Patched twin rejects malicious host param
